name: Kubernetes Baseline
description: Guards against destructive kubectl and Helm operations: namespace deletion, prod-context changes, node disruption, and secret removal. Requires admin approval via access request for the most dangerous operations; escalates others to require written justification.

rules.name: Require access request for kubectl delete on prod-named resources
rules.description: Requires admin approval for any kubectl delete targeting resources whose name contains prod, production, or prd. Guards against accidental removal of production workloads.

rules.name: Require access request for kubectl delete namespace
rules.description: Deleting a namespace irreversibly removes all resources within it. Requires admin approval before proceeding.

rules.name: Require access request for kubectl delete secret
rules.description: Deleting a Kubernetes secret could break running workloads that depend on it. Requires admin approval before proceeding.

rules.name: Require access request to operate on prod cluster context
rules.description: Any kubectl command that targets a prod-named context must be approved by an admin. Prevents accidental cross-context operations against production.

rules.name: Require access request for Helm uninstall or delete
rules.description: helm uninstall and helm delete remove deployed releases and their associated resources. Requires admin approval.

rules.name: Require justification for kubectl drain or cordon
rules.description: Draining or cordoning nodes evicts running pods and prevents new scheduling. Requires a written reason before proceeding.

rules.name: Require justification for kubectl exec into a pod
rules.description: Executing a shell in a running pod allows arbitrary code execution inside the cluster. Requires a written reason.
